correctness.notes: Theorem \ref{thm:feasible-upper-log} via Definition \ref{def:uniform-feasible-collection}: Assumption \ref{ass:overlap-decay} is included in the upper theorem because \mathcal Q must be a \kappa-uniform primitive finite-cell feasible collection, but no reproduced upper-bound step consumes P{p_P(X)\le v,0<|\tau_P(X)|\le u}\le C_o u^\alpha v^{1/\gamma}. The proof uses causal sampling/boundedness, margin localization, \pi_P^*\in\Pi and finite VC, fixed fold constants, known logging, bounded cell means, and the rare-arm cell-count envelope; it does not use the overlap-tail inequality.

perItemFindings.label: correctness
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Theorem \ref{thm:feasible-upper-log} via Definition \ref{def:uniform-feasible-collection}: Assumption \ref{ass:overlap-decay} is included in the upper theorem because \mathcal Q must be a \kappa-uniform primitive finite-cell feasible collection, but no reproduced upper-bound step consumes P{p_P(X)\le v,0<|\tau_P(X)|\le u}\le C_o u^\alpha v^{1/\gamma}. The proof uses causal sampling/boundedness, margin localization, \pi_P^*\in\Pi and finite VC, fixed fold constants, known logging, bounded cell means, and the rare-arm cell-count envelope; it does not use the overlap-tail inequality. → The upper theorem is stated under a redundant law-side overlap assumption. The reproduced proof supports the same bound for any collection satisfying the primitive finite-cell nuisance envelope and margin/VC conditions with the chosen deterministic q_n^*, regardless of overlap decay.

verbatim_critique:
D0.5.1 (math referee) found load-bearing correctness defects — re-derive the flagged step(s):
[serious, setup] Theorem \ref{thm:feasible-upper-log} via Definition \ref{def:uniform-feasible-collection}: Assumption \ref{ass:overlap-decay} is included in the upper theorem because \mathcal Q must be a \kappa-uniform primitive finite-cell feasible collection, but no reproduced upper-bound step consumes P{p_P(X)\le v,0<|\tau_P(X)|\le u}\le C_o u^\alpha v^{1/\gamma}. The proof uses causal sampling/boundedness, margin localization, \pi_P^*\in\Pi and finite VC, fixed fold constants, known logging, bounded cell means, and the rare-arm cell-count envelope; it does not use the overlap-tail inequality.. Fix: The upper theorem is stated under a redundant law-side overlap assumption. The reproduced proof supports the same bound for any collection satisfying the primitive finite-cell nuisance envelope and margin/VC conditions with the chosen deterministic q_n^*, regardless of overlap decay.

Referee notes: I reproduced the upper clipped-AIPW route, the lower weak-arm two-point route, and the corollary's dependence on those two results. The term expansions and rates reproduce, but the upper theorem carries an unused overlap-decay assumption through the feasible-collection setup.
